news.headline: The Designery's 1st Metro Atlanta location opens in Feb. Owner Alexandra & Specialist Julie aim to be top destination for designs.
news.summary: Alexandra Hauser and Julie Little aim to establish The Designery as Atlanta's premier destination for kitchen, bath, and closet designs. Located in the city, the full-service design center offers immersive experiences and top-notch services. The establishment, founded by Hauser, promises high-quality products and personalized solutions to cater to every need. The team envisions becoming the go-to one-stop shop for residents seeking expertly crafted and innovative design ideas.

news.headline: Terex reports strong 2024 results: $5.1B sales, $4.96 EPS, 10.3% operating margin, $6.11 adjusted EPS, 21.9% from ESG, $0.17 dividend.
news.summary: In the fiscal year ending in 2024, a company reported total sales reaching $5.1 billion. The full-year operating margins were at 10.3%, which increased to 11.3% when adjusted. Earnings per share for the year were $4.96, or $6.11 when adjusted. The company saw a substantial contribution from the ESG sector in the fourth quarter of 2024, with adjusted operating margins of 21.9%. Additionally, a quarterly cash dividend of $0.17 per share was announced. The company is based in Norwalk, Conn., and the announcement was made on February 6, 2025.

news.headline: CEO Warrior's Sandy Papavero and Jason Noel to speak at WWETT Show in Feb, training contractors to grow business. #EWETTShow2025 #businessgrowth
news.summary: Top consultants will educate water and wastewater contractors on how to address concerns hindering their business expansion. This initiative was revealed by CEO Warrior, a renowned entity offering comprehensive business training and coaching to service business proprietors worldwide. The announcement was made on February 6, 2025, in Eatontown, New Jersey.

news.headline: BoE: MPC will ensure that the bank rate is restrictive for sufficiently long to return inflation to the 2% target sustainably.
BoE forecasts unemployment rate in Q4 2025 4.5% (November: 4.1%}; @4 2026 4.7% (November: 4.3%); Q4 2027 4.8% (November: 4.4%)

Global chloramine filters market expected to reach US$ 3.27 billion by 2034 with 6.5% CAGR. Demand rises due to health risks and water quality concerns. Products like Catalytic Activated Carbon Filters and Reverse Osmosis Systems are in high demand. Stringent regulations and technological advancements drive market growth. North America holds significant market share. APEC Water Systems, Aquasana, Pentair, Kinetico among top players.
news.summary: The global chloramine filters market was valued at US$1,744.3 million in 2024 and is projected to reach US$3,274.2 million by 2034, growing at a notable CAGR of 6.5%. The increasing demand for chloramine filters is fueled by concerns over safe drinking water and health risks associated with chloramine exposure, which can lead to respiratory diseases and skin irritation. Catalytic activated carbon filters and reverse osmosis systems are in high demand for removing chloramines. Stringent water quality regulations, consumer awareness, and technological advancements are also driving market growth. One in five Americans uses drinking water treated with chloramines, which can produce carcinogenic by-products. North America is expected to hold a market share of 6% in 2034. Key market players include APEC Water Systems, Aquasana, Pentair (Pelican), Kinetico, and others.

news.headline: MACOM Q1 2025: Revenue $218.1M (38.8% increase y-o-y), Gross margin 53%. NASDAQ: MTSI.
news.summary: In Lowell, Massachusetts, MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: MTSI), a prominent semiconductor products provider, unveiled its financial outcomes for the fiscal first quarter concluding on January 3, 2025. The revenue for this quarter reached $218.1 million, marking a substantial surge of 38.8% from $157.1 million in the corresponding period last year, and a growth of 8.7% from $200.7 million in the previous quarter. The gross margin stood at 53%.

Warner Music Group and Spotify sign new multi-year agreement to support artists and fans, enhancing growth and innovation.

This agreement, between the two entities, solidifies their shared dedication to artists, songwriters, and fans. Warner Music Group is listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol WMG.

news.headline: Paul Verdu takes over leadership roles in AB USA and Octopi Brewing, with $35M investment.
news.summary: Waunakee, Wisconsin - On February 6, 2025, Paul Verdu was named the Managing Director of both Asahi Beer USA, Inc. (AB USA) and Octopi Brewing, LLC. The companies are under Asahi Europe & International and are aiming to expand significantly under his leadership. AB USA is responsible for promoting Asahi Super Dry, a popular product in their portfolio.

news.headline: Flimp now offers branded Total Rewards Statements for mid-market, aiming to enhance benefits appreciation and retention.
news.summary: In Boston on February 6, 2025, Flimp, a leading provider of HR communication solutions, announced that employers can now customize, highlight, and securely deliver employee compensation, perks, and benefits packages. This initiative aims to enhance the appreciation of benefits and boost employee retention.

news.headline: Garmin recognized as Top Fitness Company with first-ever HRM-Fit monitor for women in 2025.
news.summary: Garmin, a leading company in the health and wellness industry, introduces the HRM-Fit heart rate monitor specifically designed for women. This groundbreaking product has positioned Garmin among the top companies revolutionizing health and fitness. The innovative HRM-Fit was unveiled in Olathe, Kansas, on February 6, 2025, garnering attention for its unique features tailored to women's needs. This move has further solidified Garmin's stature in the market. The company, listed as GRMN on the NYSE, has proven itself as a key player with a forward-thinking approach in enhancing the health and wellness sector.

news.headline: First view among majority: Disinflation is on track after looking through near-term pickup in inflation, warrants a gradual and "careful" approach
One member who voted for a larger rate cut sought an "activist" signal on the appropriateness of UK financial conditions, and said rates still need to stay restrictive for some time
Second view among majority: Pay, inflation pressure, and weak growth reflect supply constraints, warrant gradual and "cautious" rate-cutting
BoE forecast shows CPI in two years at 2.3% (November forecast: 2.2%)
Altus Power will be acquired by TPG for $5/share in a $2.2 billion deal, making Altus largest US commercial-scale solar owner.
news.summary: Altus Power, Inc., a leading commercial-scale solar owner in the US, has revealed it will be acquired by TPG through the TPG Rise Climate Transition Infrastructure strategy. The agreement states that the acquisition will be for $5.00 per share of its Class A common stock. This all-cash transaction sets the total value of the Company at around $2.2 billion, which includes its outstanding debt. After the acquisition is finalized, Altus Power will be under TPG ownership.

news.headline: ConocoPhillips announces Q4 2024 earnings of $2.3 billion ($1.90 per share) and sets 2025 guidance and quarterly dividend.
news.summary: ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) announced their fourth-quarter 2024 earnings in Houston, reporting $2.3 billion in profits, equivalent to $1.90 per share.

news.headline: Mustang Bio prices $8 million public offering, selling 2.6M shares, each at $3.01 with accompanying warrants, expected to close Feb 7, 2025.
news.summary: Mustang Bio, Inc. has announced a public offering of 2,657,807 shares of its common stock, Series C-1 warrants for 2,657,807 shares, and Series C-2 warrants for 2,657,807 shares. The offering is priced at $3.01 per share and includes accompanying warrants with an exercise price of $3.01 per share. The Series C-1 warrants expire in five years, while the Series C-2 warrants expire in twenty-four months from the date of stockholder approval. The closing of the offering is expected on or around February 7, 2025. Mustang Bio focuses on developing cell therapies for difficult-to-treat cancers.

OneWater Marine Inc. (NASDAQ: ONEW) acquires American Yacht Group to enhance boat sales, finance, and services in Southeastern U.S.
news.summary: In Buford, Georgia, OneWater Marine Inc. (NASDAQ: ONEW) disclosed the successful acquisition of American Yacht Group, solidifying its footprint in the Southeastern U.S. This move enriches the company's range of new and pre-owned boat sales, finance, insurance, and parts and services. By integrating American Yacht Group, OneWater aims to bolster its premium yacht selection and reinforce its status in the high-end marine market. This acquisition marks a strategic step in enhancing its overall market presence and offerings.

Known for its comprehensive technology and customer management approach, Cleafy stands out in this competitive landscape.

news.headline: Mangoceuticals reports significant reduction in H1N1 viral load in Phase 1 studies, partners with Vipragen for expanded H5N1 research.
news.summary: Today, in Dallas, Texas, Mangoceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: MGRX) revealed advancements in its study on H1N1 effectiveness. The study is being carried out by Vipragen Biosciences, an AAALAC-accredited preclinical CRO in Mysuru, India, in conjunction with Intramont Technologies, Inc. The study focuses on Mangoceuticals' patented technology to prevent respiratory illnesses. Mangoceuticals is dedicated to creating and selling men’s wellness products and services through a secure telemedicine platform.

news.headline: A P Moller Maersk (OTC: AMKBY) announced Q4 earnings on Feb 6, 2025 at 04:00 AM, beating estimates. Details on Benzinga.
news.summary: On Thursday, February 6, 2025, A P Moller Maersk (OTC:AMKBY) revealed its Q4 earnings at 04:00 AM. The company surpassed estimated earnings. For more details, refer to the full report on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Meridian Flight Systems partners with Altair Aerospace Startup Acceleration Program to enhance development of Corra UAV and HERMES projects.
news.summary: Meridian Flight Systems has announced a partnership with Altair, a renowned company in computational intelligence. This collaboration aims to bolster the development of their Corra UAV and HERMES projects. The agreement, disclosed on February 6, 2025, includes the utilization of Altair software and services to enhance the project outcomes significantly. Altair, listed on the Nasdaq as ALTR, is excited about the partnership, which demonstrates their capabilities and expertise in the aerospace industry. This strategic move is expected to have a positive impact on both companies, leveraging their strengths to achieve new milestones in the field.

news.headline: Immuneering partners with Regeneron for clinical trial of IMM-1-104 with Libtayo: a promising collaboration.
news.summary: Immuneering has recently revealed an important agreement with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals regarding clinical supply. This agreement will facilitate the evaluation of IMM-1-104 alongside Libtayo® (cemiplimab). The collaboration will allow for the assessment of the potential benefits of this combination in a clinical setting. This development marks a significant step in advancing research and potentially improving treatment options.

news.headline: Conmed beats Q4 earnings estimates with $1.34 per share, $345.94 million in sales. Analysts lower forecasts for FY25.
news.summary: CONMED Corporation (NYSE:CNMD) exceeded analysts' expectations for its fourth-quarter earnings, reporting $1.34 per share compared to the estimated $1.20 per share. The company's quarterly sales also surpassed expectations at $345.94 million, above the estimated $341.01 million. However, the FY25 earnings and revenue guidance provided by Conmed fell below estimates. The CEO acknowledged the challenges faced in 2024 due to persistent supply issues but expressed satisfaction with the improved profitability in the fourth quarter. This information is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Valvoline Inc. saw 11% sales growth in Q1 2025, with an 8% SSS growth and added 35 new stores. Profit and EPS more than doubled.
news.summary: Valvoline Inc. reported a strong start to fiscal year 2025 with 11% top-line growth, 8% system-wide same-store sales (SSS) growth, and the addition of 35 new stores. The company's sales reached $414 million, with system-wide store sales at $820 million. Operating income grew 129% to $143.8 million, with income from continuing operations increasing by 177% to $93.9 million. Earnings per diluted share (EPS) rose by 181% to $0.73. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 14% to $102.8 million, and adjusted EPS grew by 10% to $0.32. Valvoline aims to expand its network to over 3,500 stores through various strategies. The company also recorded total debt of $1.0 billion and returned $39 million to shareholders through share repurchases. Valvoline aims to achieve its full-year guidance and is optimistic about its growth prospects.

Shareholders can access the shareholder letter on the Peloton investor relations website. Additionally, there will be a conference call and live audio webcast at 8:30 a.m. ET today to review the results.

news.headline: Lightspeed announced results of strategic review and Q3 2025 financials. Revenue of $280.1M, subscription revenue up 9%, net loss improved to ($26.6)M, positive Adjusted EBITDA of $16.6M, monthly ARPU grew 19% to ~$533. Board authorized $400M share repurchase. See more details at PRNewswire.
news.summary: Lightspeed Commerce Inc. recently disclosed its financial results for the three and nine months ended December 31, 2024. The company reported a total revenue of $280.1 million, up by 17% year-over-year, with subscription revenue growing by 9%. The net loss for the period improved to ($26.6) million, with positive Adjusted EBITDA of $16.6 million, exceeding expectations. Lightspeed's monthly ARPU in the quarter grew by 19% to approximately $533. The company also announced a share repurchase program to return up to $400 million to shareholders. Lightspeed has initiated a transformation plan focusing on growth in retail in North America and hospitality in Europe. Additionally, notable customer wins were reported, including multi-location merchants in various sectors. The Company expects revenue growth of approximately 20% and an Adjusted EBITDA of over $53 million for fiscal 2025. Lightspeed will also hold a Capital Markets Day on March 26, 2025, to discuss its transformation plan and financial outlook in detail. Non-IFRS financial measures were used, and the company plans to execute on its growth strategy with a strong focus on profitability and innovation.

news.headline: Hamilton Lane closes inaugural Venture Access Fund with $615M+ in commitments, exceeding target size.

The Fund focuses on top-performing funds and companies. This announcement was made on February 6, 2025, in Conshohocken, Pennsylvania.

news.headline: BioXcel Therapeutics (Nasdaq: BTAI) is implementing a 1-for-16 reverse stock split, effective Feb 7, 2025. Trading starts split-adjusted on Feb 10.

The reverse stock split will be in effect at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time on February 7, 2025, and the company’s common stock is anticipated to commence trading on a split-adjusted basis starting February 10, 2025. This move aims to adjust the stock value to potentially increase investor interest and trading activity.

news.headline: Pinpoint Predictive partners with Lightspeed Insurance to revolutionize risk selection for P&C insurers. #Precisionpricing.
news.summary: Enhancing Risk Selection and Simplifying Underwriting for Improved Efficiency in San Mateo, California, on February 6, 2025, Pinpoint Predictive, Inc., the top risk evaluation platform for Property and Casualty (P&C) insurance companies, has revealed a significant collaboration. This partnership aims to boost risk assessment accuracy and facilitate underwriting processes.

news.headline: Colliers Q4 results: Revenue $1.50B (22% increase), Adjusted EBITDA $225.3M (up 14%), Adjusted EPS $2.26 (13% increase). Full-year revenue $4.82B (11% increase). Optimistic outlook for 2025.
news.summary: Colliers International Group Inc. reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. In Q4, revenues were $1.50 billion, up 22% (22% in local currency) and Adjusted EBITDA was $225.3 million, up 14%. Full year revenues stood at $4.82 billion, up 11%, with Adjusted EBITDA at $644.2 million, up 8%. Adjusted EPS for the quarter and full year were $2.26 and $5.75 respectively. The company highlighted robust growth in all business segments, with Real Estate Services, Engineering, and Investment Management all seeing increases in revenues. Colliers anticipates continued growth in 2025, expecting high single digit to low-teens percentage revenue growth and low-teens growth in Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted EPS. The company's global Engineering platform, with over 8,000 professionals, is a key growth driver, with strong recurring revenues and growth opportunities internally and through acquisitions. Colliers maintains its commitment to accelerating client, investor, and employee success.
